vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle has a DCT dual-clutch transmission — it requires 12V power to shift reliably.
Locate the shift lock slot — Cap near gear selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
DCT REQUIRES POWER: Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If mechanical slot is used without power: DCT transmissions may resist — apply steady firm pressure and hold for several seconds.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near gear selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near gear selector base on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot: Cap near gear selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly down.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
If using slot without power, apply consistent firm pressure — hybrid selectors may require more force.
Hybrid: 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV battery.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/Hybrid: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near gear selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near gear selector base.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/Hybrid: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.warn: AWD — flatbed preferred. Block all wheels.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base
shift: Turn the Hyundai Ioniq 5 to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.notes: E-GMP platform. 800V system — NEVER touch HV components.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: 800V System — Extreme HV Hazard
knownIssues.detail: The Ioniq 5 uses an 800V high-voltage system — twice the voltage of most EVs. This is NOT a 400V system. Never touch, probe, or attempt to access any orange HV cables or components. 12V auxiliary battery under hood only.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY
knownIssues.detail: Electric AWD or RWD. Towing with wheels on the ground will damage the motors. No exceptions.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — 12V auxiliary red (+) / black (−) terminals
battery.warnings: 800V HV system — NEVER touch HV components. 12V auxiliary under hood ONLY. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: COLUMN-MOUNTED electronic selector — slot near column base
shift: Turn the Hyundai Ioniq 6 to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: COLUMN-MOUNTED electronic selector — slot near column base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: Column-Mounted Electronic Shifter
knownIssues.detail: The Ioniq 6 uses a unique column-mounted electronic gear selector — there is no center console shifter. The shift lock slot is near the base of the steering column, not on the console. Operators unfamiliar with this layout will spend time looking in the wrong place.

knownIssues.title: 800V System — Extreme HV Hazard
knownIssues.detail: Same 800V platform as Ioniq 5. 12V battery under hood only. Never touch HV components.

battery.warnings: 800V HV system. 12V auxiliary only. Column-mounted electronic shifter. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base (ICE) or slot near electronic selector (EV)
shift: Turn the Hyundai Kona to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Cap near selector base (ICE) or slot near electronic selector (EV).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
NEVER touch the high-voltage traction battery.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD/EV: flatbed only. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals. EV: 12V auxiliary only.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: Locate shift lock slot — small cap near selector base.
Pry off cap. Insert key and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.

MANUAL TRANSMISSION: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.

towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
